feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance
feed.subtitle: SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Enable SAP IAG on SAP mobile start application
entry.content:
Hi All,

Can we enable SAP IAG in the SAP Mobile Start application?

Could someone please assist with the process or provide any relevant documentation?

Thanks

Prameel

entry.title: Custom tile & catalog in S/4HANA Public Cloud (2SL-SB)
entry.content:
A customer on the S/4HANA Public Cloud 2SL - Solution Builder would like to know whether it is possible somehow to have a custom tile in S/4 with a custom catalog which could take the user to SAP IAG home screen or somehow offer this x-app navigation to new hires (without paying for S/4 licence since there are no S/4 apps used at this point). Technically after a user is authorised by IAG then the user gets an SAP role (which are licensed). 

This seems to be possible in 3SL landscape using a custom catalog, but in 2SL it is not possible to create a  custom catalog. See this blog.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Identity Federation in IAS
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have configured Azure and IAS as a proxy. Users and groups from Azure are provisioned to IAS and granted access in BTP. However, there appears to be an issue with the identity federation.

Settings in IAS:

  • Allow Identity Authentication users only = OFF
  • Identity Federation: Use Identity Authentication user store = ON
  • Default Name ID Format =  unspecified
  • Subject Name Identifier = ${corporateIdP.userPrincipalName}
  • Attributes:
    • Corporate Identity Provider = ${corporateIdP.Groups}
    • Corporate Identity Provider = ${mail}
    • Corporate Identity Provider = ${login_name}
    •  

When a user exists in Azure but is not present in IAS, they cannot log in to the SAP Cloud App.

The following error message appears:

Error: Due to a misconfiguration, the Identity Provider cannot process the response. Please contact your system administrator. Troubleshooting: You can provide your administrator with the following correlation ID:

What do you think might be causing this issue ?

Thank you for your help.

Best regards

entry.title: IAS as central user management system
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

If I understand correctly, it is recommended to use the IAS user store as the central user management system.

I have the following question for clarification:

I have configured Azure as the Idp and the IAS as a proxy. Using the IPS, the groups from Azure are provisioned to IAS. If I, for example, delete/modify a user in IAS, that user is re-provisioned to the IAS user store during the next IPS job. Or, for example, the users were still existing as shadow users in the BTP subaccounts, even though they were deleted in Azure AD

How is central user management in IAS configured?

Is this the recommended approach?

Many Thanks

entry.title: Why Identity Access Governance is not available/found in SAP Discovery Center?
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 

For some reason, I am unable to search for Identity Access Governance in SAP Discovery Center? Why? 

 

 

Regards,

Surya

entry.title: Provision users in sac
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I have two group in IAS: one group (sac_azure) provision from Azure and onw group (sac_ias) creted in IAS direkt.

How can I provision only this two groups to SAC? I have configured this  properties „sac.group.filter = displayName eq "sac_azure","sac_ias"   in the IAS Source System, But still, all users are provisioned to SAC.

Thanks in advance

Best Regards

entry.title: S4 HANA Public Cloud: Cost Center Responsible Report
entry.content:
Hello all,

We have the following user story

“As a cost center manager, I should be able to see cost center plan/actual values only for my responsible cost center”.

We have already tried to work with individual roles, which are explicitly limited to one cost center. Here, however, we have the problem that other roles that the cost center manager has, which are more comprehensive in the area of cost centers, cancel each other out.

Do any of you know of another way, add-on or similar?

entry.title: SAP IPS Read/Resync Job
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I'm still not entirely clear on this.

Specifically, when I make changes to user properties (e.g., the username has changed due to marriage) in Azure and then run the "READ" job in IAS, these changes are not provisioned to IAS. Only changes like creating a new group or user in Azure are provisioned to IAS.

However, when I run the "RESYNC" job, the new changes are transferred to IAS, but all attributes (including those I modified in IAS, as well as custom attributes) get overwritten.

How do you manage this?

Which jobs should be executed to ensure that the additional attributes I entered in IAS are not overwritten?

How can we provision only the changes from Azure?

How can we maintain attributes in IAS so that they are not overwritten during the next job?

Many Thanks for support and tips!

Best Regards

entry.title: SAP IPS: Wrong value of schemas attribute
entry.content:
Helo Experts,

When I create the source system as IAS and the target system as SAC, provisioning to SAC works fine initially. However, when I make some changes or copy the code from the SAP documentation for SCIM API 2 and  a few errors appear - "Duplicated target paths warning and I try to resolve the errors, but I am unable to fix them.

Then, when I revert to the original transformation code (the one that worked at the beginning), I reinsert it, and provisioning stops working.

I get this error:

Caused by: Could not process successfully all entities from system with name: Source IAS

error=HTTP operation failed invoking https://tenant.cloud.sap/api/v1/scim2/Users with statusCode: 400, Response: {"schemas":["urn:ietf:params:scim:api:messages:2.0:Error"],"status":"400","scimType":"invalidValue","detail":"Request is unparsable, syntactically incorrect, or violates schema. Wrong value of schemas attribute"},

content=<hidden content> - Set property ips.trace.failed.entity.content=true to display the content of failed entities

Then nothing works anymore, and I have to delete the systems and create new ones.

Please help me understand how to avoid or fix such errors."

What am I doing wrong?

Many Thanks

entry.title: SAP Identity Authentication vs. Local Identity Directory - Differences
entry.content:
Hello All,

What is the difference between SAP Identity Authentication and Local Identity Directory in SAP IPS/IAS ?

In both cases, users are stored in SAP IAS, but I don't understand the difference and when each solution should be used.

Thank you very much!

 

BEst Regards

entry.title: SAP Enable Now und IAS
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have configured IAS as a proxy and am provisioning users from Azure to IAS and then to SAP Enable Now Azure->IAS->. How can roles be automatically assigned to provisioned users in Enable Now ?

Is there something similar to groups/teams in SAP Enable Now, like in SAC, that can be granted access via a role?

Unfortunately, I can't find any information on this.

Many Thanks for any help!

entry.title: Applications, Single and Composite roles are not displayed in IAG Access request
entry.content:
Hi All,

While trying to raise a Access request in IAG, no single role, composite role is appearing to be selected. Also, if a user has a application and want to add more roles for the same application, then the same does  not appear . 

Regards

Plaban

entry.title: IPS: Email and Telephone Not Provisioned to Enable Now
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

Why are the email and phone number attributes not being provisioned  to Enable Now using the default transformation code?

I am provisioning users with SAP IPS  from Azure to IAS and then to Enable Now.

Many Thanks

entry.title: IAS to SAC: User Updates Not Provisioned
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have provisioned users from IAS to SAC.

However, when I change a user in IAS (e.g., updating the last name) and run the Read job, the changes are not provisioned to the target system (SAC).

There is no error, but the users are read, and no update is performed in the target system.

What should I pay attention to and what needs to be configured to ensure that only the user changes are successfully provisioned?

 

Many Thanks

Best regard

entry.title: SAP Commerce cloud integration with SAP IAG as SCIM system
entry.content:
Hello,

SAP Commerce cloud integration with SAP IAG as SCIM system .

IN Swagger connectivity and details are about to pull.

In IAG, getting repository synch fail with 500 error.

Please suggest.

SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance

entry.title: Best Practices for Managing Users and Groups in Azure and IAS
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

Currently, we have the following scenario implemented: We have Azure as the IdP → IAS as a proxy → Cloud Apps. The groups are created in Azure, and users are assigned to those groups in Azure. These groups with their users are then provisioned to SAP IAS. From SAP IAS, the users/groups, such as SAC_Group, are provisioned to the SAC app, or the group ENABLE_Group is provisioned to Enable Now.

Since we, as the SAP team, do not have access to the Azure tenant, I am asking if there is a better approach or any best practices for managing users/groups.

Could we provision only users from Azure and create the groups directly in IAS, then manually assign users to these groups und then provision to Cloud Apps? Are there any disadvantages to this approach?

What are your experiences? How do you handle this in your environment? What are the best practices: Should users/groups be managed in Azure, or should groups be managed directly in IAS?

Which approach is better: Managing users/groups in Azure or provisioning users to IAS and managing the groups there?

Thank you so much!

Best Regards

entry.title: IAS/IPS: UPN in Azure and UserName in Cloud Apps
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

The following problem:

As far as I know, the UPN is typically set as an email address.

In my scenario, users are provisioned from Azure -> IAS -> cloud applications.

However, when provisioning users from IAS  (for example) to Enable Now, the usernames are created as email addresses.

 

 

How can I avoid this?

For example, in Entra ID Source System, the User Name is defined in the Default Code as follows:

{
"sourcePath": "$.userPrincipalName",
"targetPath": "$.userName",
"correlationAttribute": true
},

So, this means that the UPN (Azure) becomes the Login Name ( in IAS).

For example, if I map the employee ID to the username in the IPS transformation, the user is created in Enable Now with the employee ID (which would be ideal for us) as the username, but the users cannot authenticate in Azure with the employee ID.

Is there a way to change the UPN?

Or is there another solution to ensure that the usernames in cloud applications are not created as email addresses, while users can still authenticate in Azure?

Thank you very much !!!

Best regards

entry.title: SAP IAG TandD application plan
entry.content:
Hi All,

Please let know the meaning of TandD application plan for SAP IAG

Regards

Plaban

entry.title: Error in group provisioning to commerce cloud
entry.content:
Hello All,

Error occurring in Commerce cloud group provisioning .

User able to create bit fails in group provisioning.

Please suggest

Thanks

entry.title: User Attributes Not Transferred to SAP BTP from Azure AD via IAS with OIDC
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have configured SAP IAS as a proxy and Azure as IdP, with OIDC as protocol between Azure and IAS. I am trying to log  in BAS. but I have encountered an issue- Access denied.  The User is created in SAP BTP but the user name are not transferred correctly und first und last name are not transferred at all to SAP BTP:

Configuration in IAS:

  • Subject Name Identifier: (Corporate Identity Provider) - NameID

     

  • Attributes:

     

     

Configuration in Azure:

  • The necessary claims such as are available in Azure AD.

Where could the issue be in the configuration?

Thank you in advance for your help.

Best regards.
